steps.title: Interpret the annual percentage increase
steps.content:
When a quantity increases by 4.5% each year, it retains its full value (100%) and gains an additional 4.5%. This means each year, the value is multiplied by:
$$1 + 0.045 = 1.045$$

steps.title: Build the exponential growth function
steps.content:
Starting with 181,000, after $x$ years of compound growth (multiplying by 1.045 each year):
$$f(x) = 181000(1.045)^x$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
